response:
THE CENTRAL CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM. THE NEXT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE, AND THIS PATTERN CONTINUES FOR A TOTAL OF 8 CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. CALCULATE THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES COMBINED. EXPRESS YOUR ANSWER IN TERMS OF R AND PI.

THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS EQUAL TO THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², AND THE RADIUS OF EACH CIRCLE IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS R, THE SECOND IS 1.1R, THE THIRD IS 1.21R, AND SO ON, UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE. THE TOTAL AREA IS πR² + π(1.1R)² + π(1.21R)² + π(1.331R)² + π(1.4641R)² + π(1.61051R)² + π(1.771561R)² + π(1.9487171R)².

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2π(1.4641R), AND THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER IS 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS EQUAL TO THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE GAP, AND THIS MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 144.
